company.name: Pachama
company.notes: Carbon removal AI. Mission-driven. | ACQUIRED: Acquired by Carbon Direct. Carbon Direct not yet in this DB — consider adding if you want carbon-removal-tech roles.

research.status_summary: Pachama no longer exists as a standalone entity — it was fully acquired by Carbon Direct on November 10, 2025, with its AI/satellite forest-carbon MRV platform absorbed into Carbon Direct's product suite; Pachama's own jobs page currently shows zero open roles.
research.hiring_policy: Hiring now flows through Carbon Direct, which is remote-friendly and operates across 4 countries and 10+ U.S. states with offices in Seattle and Oakland; Carbon Direct has sponsored H1B visas in the past, though sponsorship is not guaranteed per role.
recent_news: June 2025 – Pachama laid off ~20 employees (dropping headcount to ~35) as voluntary carbon markets softened under anti-ESG headwinds and shrinking corporate sustainability budgets, signaling financial distress before the sale.

November 10, 2025 – Carbon Direct acquired Pachama (undisclosed terms); Pachama co-founder/CEO Diego Saez Gil and key staff joined Carbon Direct, with Pachama's digital MRV platform integrated into Carbon Direct's advisory and diligence services.

March 31, 2026 – Crunchbase shows a paywalled news item about the combined entity, suggesting ongoing post-merger activity; pachama.com now redirects to Carbon Direct's site, confirming full brand absorption.
research.growth_signal: As part of Carbon Direct (70+ scientists, 150+ global clients, $60.8M raised), Pachama's tech is being scaled into a broader carbon management platform, but the standalone Pachama brand and headcount are shrinking/gone — growth now lives at the Carbon Direct level.
research.fit_for_lior: Weak fit as a direct job target since Pachama is absorbed and posts no open roles; Carbon Direct could be a pipeline entry for a data/product analyst or ML-adjacent role in climate tech, but it skews toward deep climate science over Creative AI or vibe-coding profiles.
